_readme: Manual, human-editable overlay applied automatically at load time (see src/data/curationRules.js, wired into jsonAdapter.getSkus). These are judgment calls a fresh data export can't know about on its own -- they persist here so a brand-new skus.json (from an updated brand ranking / MI Specific Data export) doesn't silently lose them the way the 07-16 relabel fix did in the 07-18 rebuild. Edit this file directly to add/remove rules; no code changes needed for a new entry of an existing kind.

nonAlcoholicUpcs._readme: UPC -> true moves this SKU into a brand-new NON-ALCOHOLIC varietal section, out of whatever varietal section it used to occupy. A standing, growing list -- Andrew, 2026-07-23: 'we will be putting a number of existing SKUs' here over time, not a one-off fix. Add new UPCs directly, no code change needed; the section itself needs no separate registration (sectionForSku builds a section from whatever string sku.varietal holds).
nonAlcoholicUpcs._relaxZeroReadme: Relax Zero Sauvignon Blanc (82242018081, was varietal SAUVIGNON BLANC) and Relax Zero Chardonnay (82242017871, was varietal CHARDONNAY) -- both non-alcoholic, so they no longer belong in a regular varietal section.
nonAlcoholicUpcs._freReadme: Fre (skuIds 000837, 001915, 000255, 001995, 000674, 001297, 000943, 000386, 001984, 000625, 000551, 001137) -- whole brand is alcohol-removed, per Andrew, 2026-07-23. The two 0.187LT X4 SKUs (001915, 001995) are size-routed sections already, so this varietal change doesn't move where they render, just keeps the data field accurate.
nonAlcoholicUpcs._seaglassReadme: Seaglass Alcohol Removed -- Pinot Grigio (000742), Sauvignon Blanc (000923), Sparkling Brut (003943). Andrew, 2026-07-23.
nonAlcoholicUpcs._kimCrawfordReadme: Kim Crawford Illuminate Alcohol Removed Sauvignon Blanc (001769) -- was mislabeled varietal NEW ZEALAND (the region). Andrew, 2026-07-23.

varietalRelabels._readme: UPC -> corrected varietal, for raw-export mislabels on a specific SKU where the region got put in the varietal field instead of the actual grape/style. Andrew, 2026-07-23.
varietalRelabels._stoneleighReadme: Stoneleigh (82242017051) is a Sauvignon Blanc from New Zealand -- raw source confirms 'STONELEIGH SAUVIGNON BLANC NEW ZEALAND', but skus.json had varietal set to the region (NEW ZEALAND) instead of the grape, landing it in the wrong section.
varietalRelabels._relaxReadme: Relax Pink (88474008051) is a rose sourced from Spain -- raw source confirms 'RELAX PINK SPAIN', but skus.json had varietal set to the region (SPAIN) instead of ROSE. Not the same SKU as Relax's actual Riesling (88474022401, varietal correctly RIESLING, region GERMANY) -- do not confuse the two.

sizeRelabels._readme: UPC -> canonical bottleSizeRaw. Raw MI Specific Data exports record some multi-packs by SUMMED volume (e.g. 3x187mL as '.561 LT') instead of the real per-bottle size code used everywhere else in the app. Extracted directly from the raw source 2026-07-23 (both with and without a leading zero, since skus.json's own UPC formatting is inconsistent) -- covers every known affected product in the source data, not just whichever ones happened to be in today's skus.json snapshot.

alwaysIncludeUpcs._readme: UPC -> true forces this SKU to always place in its size section, in every store, regardless of sales score. Andrew, 2026-07-22. The Coppola Prosecco 0.187LT X3 entry that used to live here (UPC 0082242018661) is now covered by the broader supplierFavoredBrands rule below (COPPOLA DIAMOND matches its brand too), so it moved out rather than being duplicated in two places.

alwaysExclude._readme: Removes matching SKUs entirely before the rest of the app ever sees them. 'brands' matches case-insensitively against the exact brand string (not a substring) -- list every brand variant a discontinued line appears under. 'upcs' is for individual discontinued SKUs within a brand that otherwise stays active.
alwaysExclude._upcsReadme: Three Finger Jack Chardonnay (82242016741) and Zinfandel (82242016881), 750ml -- discontinued per Andrew, 2026-07-23. The rest of the Three Finger Jack line (Cabernet Sauvignon, Red Blend) stays active. Relax Sparkling (88474012001, skuId 002955, 0.75LT) -- discontinued per Andrew, 2026-07-24. The rest of the Relax line (Sauvignon Blanc/Chardonnay -- actually Relax Zero, see nonAlcoholicUpcs; Relax Pink/Rose; Relax's Germany SKU) stays active.

manualAdditions._readme: Full SKU records for products that don't exist in ANY source export yet (too new to market to be tracked), keyed by a fixed skuId so re-applying this file never creates duplicates. Replace upc:null with the real UPC once available, then this entry can move out of manualAdditions (it'll already be in the real export).

supplierFavoredBrands._readme: Brand-substring match (case-insensitive) -- every matching SKU, any size, gets strategicSupplierPriority:true (scoring boost + wins the anchor/prime-position tiebreak in its section, see anchorPlacement.js). alwaysInclude:true ONLY if the entry itself sets alwaysInclude:true -- most preferred brands (matching the original hardcoded-in-skus.json list) want the boost only, same as Bota/1924/Black Stallion/etc. Not a UPC list so future new sizes/sub-lines of the same brand are covered automatically.
supplierFavoredBrands._rankReadme: Andrew, 2026-07-24: 'rank' (lower number = higher priority) breaks ties when MULTIPLE Strategic Supplier Priority brands compete for the same section's single anchor/prime-position slot -- see anchorPlacement.js. 'tier' (1 or 2) is a separate, harder rule: a tier-2 SKU may never share a physical shelf ROW with a tier-1 SKU in the same section (placementSolver.js demotes it to the next row down instead). THIS ORDER IS EXPECTED TO CHANGE -- it lives here, in data, specifically so reordering/adding/removing a brand is a JSON edit, never a code change. Brands with no rank/tier here are unranked ('the others are whatever' -- normal score-based competition only, no tiebreak preference and no shelf-separation rule).

brandContains.reason: Andrew, 2026-07-23: reverses the 2026-07-12 BUSINESS_RULES.md decision that had excluded Coppola from Strategic Supplier Priority -- explicit new instruction to favor all Francis Coppola Diamond Collection SKUs (Diamond Collection, Diamond Collection Black Label, Diamond Collection Paso Robles), every size, in every store. docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md updated to match.

Andrew, 2026-07-23: Stoneleigh was already named in the original 2026-07-12 preferred-brand list in BUSINESS_RULES.md, but had zero matching SKUs in the pool at the time so the flag was never actually live. Now has one (Stoneleigh Sauvignon Blanc, skuId 000589) -- boost only, same as every other original-list brand, not alwaysInclude.

Andrew, 2026-07-24: Bota was always a de facto Strategic Supplier Priority brand (family-ordering and 3L-majority-space rules in blocking.js/placementSolver.js predate this JSON mechanism, and this file's own readme already referenced it as a comparison case) but never actually carried the strategicSupplierPriority flag itself -- most visibly, it was excluded from the new gold-highlight UI treatment until this entry. Boost only, not alwaysInclude, since Bota already has its own dedicated placement/floor guarantees that don't depend on this flag. Matches BOTA BOX, BOTA MINI, and every sub-line (Breeze, Nighthawk, Redvolution, etc.). Rank 1 -- highest priority per Andrew's 2026-07-24 ranking.

Andrew, 2026-07-24: already a Strategic Supplier Priority brand since the original 2026-07-12 list (flag baked directly into skus.json, no curationRules entry existed for it) -- added here to give it a rank in the 2026-07-24 ranking without changing its existing boost-only behavior.
Andrew, 2026-07-24: same as Black Stallion above -- original 07-12 priority brand, added here only to carry a rank.
Andrew, 2026-07-24: same as Black Stallion above -- original 07-12 priority brand, added here only to carry a rank.

Andrew, 2026-07-24: same as Black Stallion above -- original 07-12 priority brand, added here only to carry a rank. Chardonnay/Zinfandel sub-lines were discontinued 2026-07-23 (see alwaysExclude) -- unaffected by this rank entry, which only matters for whatever's still in the pool (Cabernet Sauvignon, Red Blend).

Andrew, 2026-07-24: same as Black Stallion above -- original 07-12 priority brand, added here only to carry a rank. Matches every Relax SKU still in the pool after the 2026-07-24 Sparkling discontinuation, including the two Relax Zero (non-alcoholic) SKUs -- ranking them is harmless even though they've moved out of a normal varietal section.
Andrew, 2026-07-24: same as Black Stallion above -- original 07-12 priority brand, added here only to carry a rank.

Andrew, 2026-07-24: slightly lower priority than the rank 1-9 brands per explicit instruction -- tier 2 means this brand's SKUs may never share a physical shelf row with a tier-1 priority brand in the same section (placementSolver.js demotes to the next row down). Already a Strategic Supplier Priority brand since 2026-07-12 (flag baked into skus.json); added here for rank/tier only.
Andrew, 2026-07-24: tier 2, same reasoning as Noble Vines above.
Andrew, 2026-07-24: tier 2, same reasoning as Noble Vines above.

supplierFavoredUpcs._readme: UPC -> true (or an object with rank/tier) grants strategicSupplierPriority ONLY (scoring/anchor-tiebreak boost, not alwaysInclude) -- for a single SKU Andrew wants favored without force-placing it regardless of score, unlike the brand-wide supplierFavoredBrands rule above. Plain 'true' means no rank/tier (unranked, same as an unlisted brand for tiebreak/shelf-separation purposes, just still scored higher).

varietalOverrides._readme: Forces sku.varietal for every SKU whose brand contains 'match' (case-insensitive substring, not exact) -- only affects 750mL SKUs, since that's the only size sectionForSku keys off varietal instead of bottleSizeRaw. Brand-substring match (not a UPC list) so future new flavors of the same line get corrected automatically without needing to know their UPC in advance.

brandContains.reason: Andrew, 2026-07-23: Il Conte Stella Rosa is a sweet/flavored line despite being from Italy -- raw data mostly labels it varietal ITALY or SPARKLING (routing into the Sparkling Wine section), neither of which is right for the whole brand. All SKUs, all sub-lines (Il Conte, plain Stella Rosa, Organic, Assorted).

Andrew, 2026-07-23: all Schmitt Sohne 750ml SKUs belong in the Germany section by country of origin, not split out by grape -- most were already varietal GERMANY, but Riesling (skuId 002294) was still varietal RIESLING. Brand-wide so any future new Schmitt Sohne 750ml SKU routes to Germany automatically.
